OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION; RESPONSE TO DEFT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS; PLTF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF ADDITIONAL UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS DECLARATIONS OF AJODHYA CHOWDRY, CARY D. ALBERSTSONE, M.D. AND DAVID C. KADIN; OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE FILED BY PLAINTIFF CHOWDRY, AJODHYA CHOWDRY, URMIL

RESPONSE TO PLTFS' SEPARATE STATEMENT OF ADDITIONAL UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT ZUCHERMAN MD, JAMES F SAN FRANCISCO ORTHOPAEDIC SURGEONS MEDICAL GROUP, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS JAMES ZUCHERMAN AND SAN FRANCISCO ORTHOPAEDIC SURGEONS MEDICAL GROUP, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BATTERY - THE MOTION IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN. THE EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT THE FUSION SURGERY PERFORMED ON PLAINTIFF WAS MATERIALLY DIFFERENT FROM THE OPERATION THAT PLAINTIFF CONSENTED TO, I.E., THE X-STOP PROCEDURE. THE DECLARATION OF DR. CHOU DOES NOT CLEARLY SHOW THAT THE FUSION SURGERY WAS MEDICALLY NECESSARY BECAUSE AN EMERGENCY WAS AT HAND. DR. CHOU STATES THAT THE DR. ZUCHERMAN BELIEVED THAT THE FUSION SURGERY WOULD BENEFIT PLAINTIFF (PAGE 6, LINE 5-7); THAT PLAINTIFF'S HEALTH WAS ENDANGERED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S SPINE WAS DESTABILIZED (PG. 7:19-20), FUSION SUGERY COULD HAVE OCCURRED IN THE FUTURE (PG. 7:28, AND WITH THE FUSION PLAINTIFF'S CONDITION WOULD HAVE PROGRESSED TO WORSENING. (PG. 8:507) IN OTHER WORDS, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE FUSION SURGERY WAS NECESSARY GIVEN THE EXIGENT CIRCUMSTANCES OR WHETHER IT WAS MERELY ADVISABLE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID C. KADIN, TEL. NO. (310) 798-2271 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JAMES ZUCHERMAN AND SAN FRANCISCO ORTHOPAEDIC SURGEONS MEDICAL GROUP, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BATTERY - The motion is denied because defendant failed to shift the burden. The evidence demonstrates that the fusion surgery performed on plaintiff was materially different from the operation that plaintiff consented to, i.e., the X-Stop procedure. The declaration of Dr. Chou does not clearly show that the fusion surgery was medically necessary because an emergency was at hand. Dr. Chou states that the Dr. Zucherman believed that the fusion surgery would benefit plaintiff (page 6, line 5-7); that plaintiff's health was endangered because plaintiff's spine was destabilized (pg. 7:19-20), fusion sugery could have occurred in the future (pg. 7:28, and with the fusion plaintiff's condition would have progressed to worsening. (pg. 8:507) In other words, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the fusion surgery was necessary given the exigent circumstances or whether it was merely advisable. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
